req.title: Run payroll — variant 5
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path monthly payroll run producing completed state, pay-stubs, and audit entry with valid 2FA (EP); (2) conflict rejection when a run is already in progress (NEG/DT); (3) atomic rollback on internal failure (NEG/STC); (4) boundary of exactly one active employee — minimum non-empty workforce (BVA); (5) state transition from idle→in-progress→completed (STC). Each test is isolated via fixture teardown and uses a fresh payroll-period token to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_completes_with_pay_stubs_and_audit
preconditions: At least two active employees exist
No payroll run in progress for the period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period, 2FA OTP, and hr-admin JWT
expected: pay_stubs array contains one entry per active employee
Each stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log entry created for this run_id
trace: R-WEB-076: payroll computes gross, deductions, net per active employee
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_happy_path_completes_with_pay_stubs_and_audit(
    hr_admin_client, valid_2fa_otp, active_employees_fixture, unique_period
):
    payload = {
        "period": unique_period,
        "otp": valid_2fa_otp()
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "completed"
    stubs = body["pay_stubs"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(active_employees_fixture)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]
    run_id = body["run_id"]
    audit = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/audit-log?run_id={run_id}")
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["count"] >= 1

tests.name: test_payroll_run_rejected_409_when_run_already_in_progress
preconditions: A payroll run for the same period is currently in_progress (seeded via fixture)
actions: Seed an in-progress payroll run for target period via seed_in_progress_run fixture
POST /api/payroll-runs with same period and valid 2FA OTP
trace: R-WEB-076 error pathway: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_rejected_409_when_run_already_in_progress(
    hr_admin_client, valid_2fa_otp, seed_in_progress_run, unique_period
):
    seed_in_progress_run(period=unique_period)
    payload = {
        "period": unique_period,
        "otp": valid_2fa_otp()
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "PAYROLL_RUN_IN_PROGRESS"
    runs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs?period={unique_period}&state=completed"
    )
    assert runs.status_code == 200
    assert runs.json()["count"] == 0

tests.name: test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee_boundary
preconditions: Exactly one active employee exists for the period (minimum non-empty partition)
actions: Ensure workforce has exactly one active employee via single_employee_fixture
POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period and 2FA OTP
expected: Single stub has gross_pay > 0 and net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
trace: R-WEB-076: computes pay for each active employee — boundary at count=1
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee_boundary(
    hr_admin_client, valid_2fa_otp, single_employee_fixture, unique_period
):
    payload = {
        "period": unique_period,
        "otp": valid_2fa_otp()
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "completed"
    stubs = body["pay_stubs"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1
    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
    assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]

tests.name: test_payroll_run_500_internal_failure_rolls_back_atomically
preconditions: Fault injection configured to trigger mid-run computation error via inject_payroll_fault fixture
actions: Enable fault injection so payroll computation fails mid-run
POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period and 2FA OTP
Query database for partial pay-stub records and run state
expected: No pay-stub records persisted for the period
State never transitions to completed — atomic rollback confirmed
trace: R-WEB-076 error pathway: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-076: failed run shall not partially commit
R-WEB-076 STC: idle -> in_progress -> (fault) -> rolled_back, not completed

tests.name: test_payroll_run_rejected_when_2fa_otp_is_invalid
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period but an invalid OTP value
expected: Response body contains error related to 2FA failure
trace: R-WEB-076: action requires two-factor authentication confirmation
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_rejected_when_2fa_otp_is_invalid(
    hr_admin_client, active_employees_fixture, unique_period
):
    payload = {
        "period": unique_period,
        "otp": "000000"
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
           "otp" in body.get("message", "").lower() or \
           "authentication" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    runs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs?period={unique_period}"
    )
    assert runs.status_code == 200
    assert runs.json()["count"] == 0
